notes: Lesson 19 Orientation (act 108), 'Heap — the family map', 818.5s / ~13.6min, 7 designed cues spread over the real overview duration. Cue00 the problem (repeated min under change -> O(qn) full-scan waste). Cue01 the invariant (local parent<=children promise forces the global min to the root, but only loosely ordered). Cue02 stored as a flat array (parent(i)=(i-1)//2, left 2i+1, right 2i+2 — index arithmetic, no pointers). Cue03 the two operations (insert drop-at-end + sift up; remove pop-root + move-last-leaf + sift down; each = one root-to-leaf path = O(log n)). Cue04 applications family map (size-k top-k, k-way merge, priority scheduling, streaming median with two heaps). Cue05 Python heapq min-only + negation trick. Cue06 when NOT to use (one-shot extreme -> a scan wins; no full order; no middle lookups). Uses heap.py + arrays.py idiom libs; MathTex for the bounds. Each long cue stages reveals via wait_until so the frame keeps changing; pace_to pins the tail. Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke every cue (fixed safe-area: bottom captions need buff>=0.55; fixed cue02 index-formula/tree collision by shifting the tree+array LEFT when formulas reveal), then full -ql render (7 chunks 854x480x15 niced/serial) + READ start/mid/end for all 7 cues. Iteration 2 fixed cue03 upnote/downnote colliding with tree nodes (repositioned to fixed clear spots [2.7,-2.15]/[-2.7,-2.15]); re-rendered cue03 + re-verified both insert (extract @393s) and remove (@442s) beats clean. cue04/05-type _end boundary frames can show the next cue's opening title (documented keyframe-seek artifact); settled states verified via mid frames. Per-cue checklist (legibility, math/algorithm correctness, highlight matches the beat, contrast, safe area via theme.guard, chunk timing): all pass. Final -ql 818.269s vs audio 818.5s (delta 0.231s).
